The sequence names indicate the virus names (abbreviated) and the sequence accessions. To sort properly by the different genera, I will create a table from the names and merge that with isolate metadata that is also available via ICTV.

::: {.cell}

:::


The thing with ELM is, I could submit each sequence via the API, but would have to limit to 1 request per minute. Makes 3 hours...Guess I will let that run in the background, as well. But I also run ELM on the narrower region around the motif: 

http://elm.eu.org/start_search/


::: {.cell}

:::


# 
comment: need to integrate gget here. Did not work, takes too much time. Did it manually. 

The sequence was queried in the eukaryotic linear motif database (http://elm.eu.org/; https://academic.oup.com/nar/article/52/D1/D442/7420098). 

Screenshots of the output are provided in images/. 

![Linear Motifs in concatenated polypeptide sequences of homologous CI fragments from different species of Potyviridae](../images/ELM_graphics.png)
The results table was exported and is included in ../data/ELM_table.txt

[Linear Motifs in concatenated polypeptide sequences of homologous CI fragments from different species of Potyviridae](http://elm.eu.org/data/QiKNoNehtR/QiKNoNehtR.png)





::: {.cell}
